channel.title: AI Firehose: What Is New
channel.description: We read the whole AI firehose so you do not have to, then distill it into one brief: what is new, what is breaking out, and what is worth your time, across the day, week, month, and quarter. You take what you want and leave the rest.

item.title: Automating workflows and file organization with Antigravity
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Antigravity is a tool for automating file system workflows based on time boxes and tags. It helps developers organize GitHub repositories and local files across multiple projects, reducing time spent on file management and mental fatigue.

item.title: GPT 5.6 is out… but not for you lol
item.description: David Ondrej: David Ondrej discusses the release of GPT 5.6, highlighting that it is not yet widely available to the general public. The video appears to cover the gap between the announcement and actual user access to the model.

item.title: Use These 17 Claude Plugins, It Will Make You 10x Better.
item.description: Chase AI: Chase AI reviews 17 plugins and tools for Claude including design, productivity, and data integration options. Featured tools span Playwright, GitHub CLI, Firecrawl, LightRAG, Stripe CLI, and specialized skills for Claude code generation and task automation.

item.title: Stop Writing Tone Instructions. Layer Them. - Isadora Martin-Dye, Isadora & Co
item.description: AI Engineer: The talk presents a four-layer architecture for sustaining brand voice in AI products: immutable identity, situational mode, example-anchored voice, and deterministic post-generation veto. This approach is contrasted with single-pass tone instructions, using examples from wedding venue, companion AI, and missing persons tools.

item.title: The hidden system behind every Renaissance trial - Ada Palmer
item.description: Dwarkesh Patel: Dwarkesh Patel interviews historian Ada Palmer about systemic patterns underlying Renaissance judicial trials. The discussion explores hidden structures and institutional mechanisms that shaped trial outcomes during the Renaissance period.

item.title: System Design for Beginners (Full Guide)
item.description: AWS Developers: This tutorial presents a system design interview methodology that reverses conventional approaches by prioritizing problem requirements over technology selection. The framework consists of seven sequential steps: understanding the problem, identifying requirements, predicting traffic patterns, designing high-level components, defining APIs, selecting a tech stack, and considering implementation constraints.

item.title: Big Technology AI Summit (full): Greg Brockman, Mike Krieger, Aaron Levie & Friends of The Podcast
item.description: Alex Kantrowitz: At the inaugural Big Technology AI Summit in June 2026, industry leaders including Greg Brockman and Mike Krieger discussed the implications of the federal government's recall of Anthropic's Fable and Mythos models. Panelists debated whether the recall represents an effective "AI pause," infrastructure buildout realities, enterprise adoption challenges, and geopolitical competition with China over AI leadership.

item.title: What does the next training paradigm look like?
item.description: Dwarkesh Patel: Dwarkesh Patel discusses the next training paradigm beyond current supervised learning and RLHF approaches. Topics include grindability versus verifiability, reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR), test-time compute through dreaming, and what AI training will look like by 2027.

item.title: TEN Framework: Voice AI That Can Actually Be Interrupted
item.description: Better Stack: TEN Framework is an open-source framework for building real-time voice AI agents with streaming audio, interruption handling, VAD, turn detection, STT, LLM, TTS, avatars, telephony, and multimodal workflows. It addresses the pain point of natural conversation handling through graph-based extensions and real-time audio pipelines. The framework aims to solve production readiness issues where conventional voice agents fail under interruptions and overlapping speech.

item.title: 5 things every AI agent harness needs
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Muhammad Farooq presents five critical components required in an AI agent harness to move from prototypes to production systems. The harness is framed as the execution layer that transforms model predictions into completed tasks, distinct from the underlying AI model itself.

item.title: Gemini Canvas Tutorial: How to Use Google's Most Underrated AI Tool
item.description: AI Master: Gemini Canvas is a feature within Google's Gemini that builds working websites, no-code apps, dashboards, and audio podcasts from text prompts. The tutorial demonstrates six capability levels from document editing to advanced feature combinations.

item.title: Learn how Professor David J. Malan realized computer science is the field for him.
item.description: CS50: A YouTube video from Harvard's CS50 course in which Professor David J. Malan shares his personal journey to discovering computer science as his field. The video is part of CS50, a widely-distributed introduction to computer science and programming offered through multiple platforms including edX, Harvard Extension School, and OpenCourseWare.

item.title: Turn 10,994 Notes Into Memory - Paul Iusztin, Decoding AI & Louis-François Bouchard, Towards AI
item.description: AI Engineer: Paul Iusztin and Louis-François Bouchard present an AI Research OS that converts 10,000+ notes from Obsidian vaults into live context for AI agents through three-layer memory architecture: raw content, indexed layer, and wiki layer. The system is designed to enable personalized deep research across personal knowledge bases and the open web while remaining token-efficient.

item.title: Ornith (35B,9B) + Hermes,Zed: THE FULLY PRIVATE LOCAL AGENT is ACTUALLY HERE!
item.description: AICodeKing: Ornith is a new family of open-source agentic coding models from DeepReinforce, ranging from 9B to 397B parameters and built on Gemma and Qwen. The models are designed for coding, tool use, and agentic workflows, with MIT licensing and GGUF format support for local deployment. The 35B and 9B variants are highlighted as practical for local and server use.

item.title: How to Create a Client Pitch With AI — From Prompt to PDF in 15 Minutes
item.description: AI Master: A demonstration of Venngage AI's one-pager generator that produces client-ready marketing documents from prompts. The workflow covers content generation, design, editing, visuals, and PDF export in approximately 15 minutes.

item.title: Agents in Production: How OpenGov Built and Scaled OG Assist - Gabe De Mesa, OpenGov
item.description: AI Engineer: Gabe De Mesa from OpenGov presents a talk on building and scaling AI agents in production for government workflows. The session covers agent architecture, feedback and evaluation, long context handling, monitoring, tool integration, and development practices using Effect-TS and Typescript with Claude and Cursor.

item.title: A Genius With Amnesia - Victor Savkin, Nx
item.description: AI Engineer: Victor Savkin argues that current coding agents lack context persistence and codebase visibility, limiting their utility despite technical capability. He frames this as a critical gap that needs solving and introduces Nx and Polygraph as solutions for enabling agent autonomy in monorepos.

item.title: Fable 5 Returns... This Is Big
item.description: AI Revolution: An AI industry commentator reports signals suggesting Claude Fable 5 may return after being pulled over national security concerns, citing leaked usage strings, Amazon Bedrock listings, Congressional deadlines, and shifting US-Anthropic relations. The analysis frames the potential return as significant because it would coincide with government scrutiny and a stronger Mythos successor already in development.

item.title: The Log Is The Agent - Ishaan Sehgal, Omnara
item.description: AI Engineer: Ishaan Sehgal argues that agent identity should be defined by session logs rather than models, frameworks, or tools. As LLMs become commoditized, durable append-only logs become the key differentiator, enabling properties like persistence, continuity, forkability, and portability across different models and runtimes.

item.title: Recursive Coding Agents - Raymond Weitekamp, OpenProse
item.description: AI Engineer: Recursive Language Models (RLMs) enable inference-time compute for coding agents to achieve higher performance and reliability. The approach maps RLM principles onto agentic systems, including implementations like Claude Code Dynamic Workflows and OpenProse, to guide agents through recursively solving complex tasks with declarable outcomes.

item.title: When Forgiveness Was a Trap - Ada Palmer
item.description: Dwarkesh Patel: Dwarkesh Patel interviews historian Ada Palmer on the concept of forgiveness in historical contexts, examining when forgiveness functioned as a social trap or mechanism. The discussion explores philosophical and historical dimensions of forgiveness as a social practice.

item.title: GTA VI + GPT-6: Rockstar's AI Mechanics Revealed
item.description: AI Master: A YouTube video examining claims about GTA VI using GPT-6, arguing instead that Rockstar has built proprietary real-time behavioral simulation systems based on patents and job postings. The creator rates the reliability of leaked information and compares GPT-6, Claude 5, and Gemini 3.5 capabilities within their own platform.

item.title: What's new with data agents
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud Tech announces enhanced data analytics agents with built-in capabilities for data engineering, data science, and conversational analytics. New developer tools include the Agent Development Kit (ADK) and Model Context Protocol (MCP) integrations, accessible via Gemini CLI, Claude Code, Codex, and VSCode.

item.title: Production Evals For Agentic AI Systems - Nishant Gupta, Meta Superintelligence Labs
item.description: AI Engineer: Nishant Gupta from Meta discusses production evaluation approaches for autonomous AI agents that go beyond offline benchmarks. The talk covers continuous evaluation pipelines, online vs offline evaluation architectures, human-in-the-loop systems, and methods for detecting drift and hallucinations in agentic workflows at scale.

item.title: Build Systems, Not Code - Angie Jones, Agentic AI Foundation
item.description: AI Engineer: Angie Jones argues that AI coding agents shift engineering focus from line-by-line code writing to system-level design. She contends that core engineering disciplines like systems thinking, decomposition, and state management remain essential, moving craftsmanship up a layer rather than eliminating it.

item.title: Anthropic's Labs Lead On Fable's Capabilities + Building AI-Native Products — With Mike Krieger
item.description: Alex Kantrowitz: Mike Krieger, head of Anthropic Labs and Instagram co-founder, discusses Fable and Mythos, Anthropic's frontier models that were pulled from the market following government action. Krieger shares observations on how Fable changes development workflows and addresses Anthropic's approach to safety, platform positioning, and competitive dynamics in AI product building.

item.title: Power intelligent agents with AI-native databases
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud advocates that AI-native databases are essential for powering intelligent agents with enterprise data. The session covers AI-powered search, natural language interfaces, multimodal queries, and graph RAG as means to provide agents with high-fidelity interconnected context, alongside open standards like MCP and agentic IDEs for streamlined deployment.

item.title: Boost AI context with hybrid search in Spanner
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud Spanner now supports hybrid search combining full-text and vector search capabilities. The tool enables building AI-powered applications with multi-model queries, contextual search, and RAG applications integrated with Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform.

item.title: Agent development and AgentOps with BigQuery, ADK, and MCP
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud presented Agent Development Kit (ADK) and Model Context Protocol (MCP) as standardized integration methods for building agents that connect to multiple data sources including BigQuery, Google Maps, and AlloyDB. The session also covered agent operations (AgentOps) for monitoring agent performance and costs with minimal implementation overhead.

item.title: Build connected AI: Orchestrate tools and agents with registries and ADK
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud Tech presents the Agent Development Kit (ADK) with Agent Registry for orchestrating tools and agents across organizational systems. The session covers discovering and integrating MCP servers, building custom MCP servers, and connecting agents to third-party SaaS and on-premises data sources.

item.title: What's new with Gemini from Google DeepMind
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google DeepMind announces updates to Gemini 3, featuring upgraded model reasoning, expanded context window processing, and enhanced multimodal tool use. The session showcases workflows for building and scaling agentic applications through Vertex AI, with research advancements in agent design.

item.title: Building an AI app: A low-code guide for small teams
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud Tech demonstrates a low-code approach to building AI applications for small teams without extensive AI expertise. The approach leverages corporate data via Cloud SQL and AlloyDB for PostgreSQL, using Gemini to enable natural language interfaces, hybrid search, image search, and recommendation capabilities with auto-generated code.

item.title: What's new in Looker: Empowering business users in the governed agentic era
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud presented advancements in Looker and Gemini for business intelligence, including dashboard agents that automate workflows, a semantic layer for grounding AI, conversational analytics, and embedded analytics capabilities. The platform aims to empower business users in an agentic era through governed data access and shared pre-trained agents.

item.title: NoSQL for modern apps and AI: The future of Memorystore, Firestore, and Bigtable
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud's NoSQL database services (Memorystore, Firestore, and Bigtable) are advancing to support modern AI and agentic applications. The session covers architectural innovations and scalable solutions designed for high-performance AI workloads.

item.title: Implementing DeepMind innovation: Deep research API
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google DeepMind presents the Deep research API, an offering for developers to implement agent-powered techniques using Gemini. The session demonstrates how to use Google Cloud infrastructure to integrate these agentic research capabilities into applications.

item.title: What's new in Cloud Run
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud announced new Cloud Run capabilities designed to simplify deployment of AI agents and inference workloads. The updated serverless platform offers improved efficiency, flexibility, and operational efficiency for scaling applications.

item.title: What's new in Cloud SQL: Drive performance, high availability, and security
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud announced AI-driven capabilities for Cloud SQL including automated health management and fleet-wide insights. The service supports MySQL, PostgreSQL, and SQL Server with focus on performance optimization, high availability, and security for mission-critical applications.

item.title: What's new in Google Cloud's agent platform
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud announced updates to its agent platform, including a unified Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform with an Agent Builder featuring no-code/low-code design. The platform includes a new Agent Registry and marketplace for reusable components, enterprise governance with time-travel debugging, observability, evaluation, and managed simulation capabilities.

item.title: The Miranda Hypothesis: How Hamilton Poisoned Persona Evals - Jacob E. Thomas, Results Gen
item.description: AI Engineer: Jacob E. Thomas argues that existing persona-evaluation benchmarks fail to detect "Miranda distortion," where cultural representations in training data overwhelm primary documentary records, causing AI systems to produce culturally composite personas rather than record-grounded ones. He proposes a shift from cognitive to epistemic simulation evals using domain experts and pre-registered protocols like the Prism Experiment to catch anachronistic reasoning that automated metrics miss.

item.title: What's new for AI on GKE: Training, serving, and agents
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Kubernetes Engine has added innovations for AI training, serving, and agentic workflows. The platform now supports reinforcement learning and complex AI workloads with improved reliability, scalability, and cost-effectiveness.

item.title: Engineering the future of Kubernetes for AI at scale
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud discusses Kubernetes evolution for AI-scale workloads, emphasizing hardware-aware platforms, infrastructure elasticity, agentic workflow security, and resource optimization. Google frames itself as the leading Kubernetes contributor steering industry direction for 11 consecutive years.

item.title: Unsure how to approach CS50? Carter Zenke answers.
item.description: CS50: CS50 is Harvard University's foundational computer science course available through edX, Harvard Extension School, Harvard Summer School, and OpenCourseWare. The course is led by David J. Malan and maintains active communities across multiple social media and collaboration platforms including Discord, GitHub, and Stack Exchange.

item.title: Kimi K2.7 Code: THIS is VERY CRAZY MAN!
item.description: AICodeKing: Moonshot AI released Kimi K2.7 Code, a coding-focused agentic model with Mixture-of-Experts architecture and one trillion total parameters. It supports a 256K context window and includes Kimi Code CLI for terminal-based repository exploration, file editing, command execution, and testing.

item.title: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 Day 3 Livestream
item.description: AI Engineer: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 held its final day of main-stage programming on July 2, 2026 at Moscone West in San Francisco. The livestream featured keynotes, talks, and a startup battlefield competition, serving as a major industry gathering point.

item.title: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 Day 2 Livestream
item.description: AI Engineer: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 is a conference held at Moscone West in San Francisco on July 1, 2026. The event features keynote sessions and main-stage programming throughout the day, livestreamed online.

item.title: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 Day 1: Software Factories & Keynotes
item.description: AI Engineer: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 Day 1 livestream from San Francisco features keynote sessions and main-stage programming on software factories and AI engineering topics. The event runs June 30, 2026 at Moscone West with keynotes at 9:00-10:30 AM and 4:30-5:30 PM PT, plus main programming sessions throughout the day.

item.title: Anthropic accuses Alibaba of illicitly extracting AI capabilities - BBC
item.description: Google News AI: Anthropic has accused Alibaba of illicitly extracting AI capabilities, according to a BBC report. The accusation suggests unauthorized access to or replication of AI model functionality.

item.title: Ask HN: Where is our profession (programmer) going?
item.description: syntaxbush: A programmer observes that at a visited software company, LLMs like Claude have become the primary tool for code generation and problem-solving, with human code review, abstraction practices, and deep problem understanding being de-emphasized. The author expresses concern that this represents a shift from precise, knowledge-intensive work toward probabilistic, outsourced decision-making.

item.title: The Agentic OS Setup That Will 10x Claude Code
item.description: Chase AI: Chase AI argues that mastering Agentic OS fundamentals improves Claude Code outputs through better skills, loop engineering, and memory management. The presenter claims an Agentic OS setup can "10x" Claude Code productivity while enabling extreme customization, visual interfaces, and team collaboration.

item.title: [AINews] It's Meta-Harness Summer
item.description: Latent Space: Latent Space discusses a shift toward Meta's harness approaches for evaluating AI systems, suggesting Meta-based testing harnesses are becoming the dominant standard in the industry. The piece presents this as a significant trend shift in AI evaluation methodology.
